title: East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 5.1 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.15.33: East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.33' running on pan:traffic with src_zone, dest_zone, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
description: Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.
value: Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.
implementation: (1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry.
visualization: Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We catch flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
```

Understanding this SPL

**East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)** ‚Äî Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: fw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=fw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_zone, dest_zone, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)** ‚Äî Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week).
